items.title: Show HN: NUA an agent that tests for product correctness
items.why_it_matters: A team has shipped NUA, an agent designed to test for product correctness by turning requirements (like from PDFs) into actual tests. This is a clear example of someone building and shipping a real product with agents, showing how to direct AI to solve a specific problem (reliable testing) and addressing common issues with AI-generated tests. You can learn from their approach to directing agents. Concrete action: You could upload a PDF of your AI-ingest project's requirements or a feature spec to NUA this week to see how it generates tests for product correctness.

items.title: Show HN: 2-command CLI to give AI agents structured data retrieval on PostgreSQL
items.why_it_matters: Lithium is a new open-source CLI tool that allows AI agents to easily store and retrieve structured, hierarchical data directly from your existing PostgreSQL database. Since you use Supabase (which is PostgreSQL-based) and are learning to direct AI agents, this tool directly plugs into your current backend stack and gives your agents a powerful way to manage structured information, which is often more precise than vector stores for certain data. Concrete action: You could try installing Lithium this week and connecting it to a local Supabase instance to experiment with giving a Claude agent structured data access for your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Show HN: Tok - A Claude Code token counter (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required)
items.why_it_matters: Tok is a new, open-source tool that lets you count tokens for Claude Code prompts and responses locally, without needing an API key. You use Claude Code daily, and managing token usage is key to understanding and reducing costs, which is a high-priority area for you. This tool gives you immediate feedback on prompt efficiency directly in your workflow. Concrete action: Install Tok this week and integrate it into your Claude Code workflow to start seeing real-time token counts for your prompts and responses.

items.title: Ask HN: Anyone else seeing serious degradation in DX with Opus 4.8?
items.why_it_matters: There are recent reports on Hacker News from developers experiencing a degradation in user experience with Claude Opus 4.8, including unexpected behavior and issues with model reliability. Claude is your primary AI API for coding, and any changes to its performance or reliability directly impact your ability to direct AI effectively and ship projects. Being aware of these reports can help you anticipate potential issues. Concrete action: This week, be more vigilant when using Claude Code, especially with Opus 4.8, and consider running a small, specific test to see if you encounter any of the reported issues yourself.

items.title: A per-project open-source Claude Code skill manager
items.why_it_matters: SkillKit is a new open-source tool that allows you to manage Claude Code skills on a per-project basis. As you get better at directing AI, managing specific skills for different projects can streamline your workflow and make your AI interactions more precise. This helps you organize your AI direction, which is your core learning goal. Concrete action: Explore SkillKit this week and consider setting up a basic skill for your AI-ingest project to see how it improves your Claude Code interactions.

items.title: Claude Code OS: open-source operations copilot that self-updates
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code OS is an open-source 'operations copilot' designed to self-update and assist with various coding operations within Claude Code. This tool aims to make your Claude Code experience more efficient and autonomous, potentially freeing you up to focus more on high-level direction rather than repetitive coding tasks. It's another way to enhance your primary development environment. Concrete action: Install Claude Code OS this week and try using it for a common operational task in your AI-ingest project, like setting up a new component or managing dependencies.

items.title: Strava for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Straude is a new tool that aims to be like 'Strava for Claude Code,' tracking your coding activity and progress within the Claude environment. You're in an 'over-learning, under-shipping' phase, and tracking your actual building time can be a powerful motivator. Visualizing your coding activity could help push you toward more 'ship sessions' and less 'tutorial time.' Concrete action: Sign up for Straude this week and connect it to your Claude Code usage to start tracking your daily coding activity and visualize your progress.

items.title: CodeGuilds – community registry for Claude Code (skills, agents, MCP servers)
items.why_it_matters: CodeGuilds is a new community registry for Claude Code skills, agents, and MCP servers. This could be a central place to discover new tools, agents, or even other developers' projects that use Claude Code, potentially offering inspiration or ready-to-use components that align with your stack or goals. It helps you see what others are building. Concrete action: Browse CodeGuilds this week to see if there are any existing Claude Code skills or agent examples that could be directly useful or inspiring for your AI-ingest project.
